title: Listening - Part 3: Favourite Cafés
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a café they visit regularly. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the café. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a café they visit regularly. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the café. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about a café they visit regularly.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I'm taking an online course alongside my job, and working at home isn't ideal because my neighbours are renovating their flat. I tried the library, but I sometimes need to join discussions online. [19]The café I use has a quiet upstairs section where I can sit with my laptop for an hour or two without music or constant conversation distracting me.[/19] I always order lunch, of course, and the staff don't mind me staying until I've completed whatever task I set myself.||Speaker 2 Man: Since discovering I can't eat dairy products, I often find cafés disappointing. They may have one suitable biscuit, but nothing I could call a proper meal. My sister mentioned a place near her office. [20]I now go there regularly because the menu clearly identifies dairy-free dishes, and there are several cakes and lunch options I can safely enjoy rather than simply settling for tea.[/20] It is slightly out of my way, but having a real choice makes the journey worthwhile.||Speaker 3 Woman: My train arrives in town about twenty minutes before my morning class begins. That's not enough time to go shopping or visit friends, but it is too long to stand outside in winter. [21]The café I use is immediately opposite the station and on the same road as my college, so I can have breakfast there without risking being late.[/21] The coffee is fine, although I probably wouldn't make a special trip for it; it's the location that makes me a regular customer.||Speaker 4 Man: There are more fashionable cafés in the town centre, with impressive cakes and modern furniture, but I keep returning to a small family-run place. I started going there when I was new in the area. [22]The owners remembered my name after two visits, ask how my week has gone and always make time to chat if I'm alone.[/22] Sometimes I go in intending only to buy a takeaway drink and end up staying for half an hour because the atmosphere is so friendly.||Speaker 5 Woman: I look after my young nephew on Saturday mornings, and taking him to indoor cafés can be difficult because he gets bored quickly. I found one at the edge of a local park. [23]Its large garden has tables under trees and plenty of safe space nearby, so he can move around and watch birds while I sit outside with a drink.[/23] In bad weather we don't go, but on warm mornings it is much more relaxing than trying to keep him quiet indoors.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I'm taking an online course alongside my job, and working at home isn't ideal because my neighbours are renovating their flat. I tried the library, but I sometimes need to join discussions online. The café I use has a quiet upstairs section where I can sit with my laptop for an hour or two without music or constant conversation distracting me. I always order lunch, of course, and the staff don't mind me staying until I've completed whatever task I set myself.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Since discovering I can't eat dairy products, I often find cafés disappointing. They may have one suitable biscuit, but nothing I could call a proper meal. My sister mentioned a place near her office. I now go there regularly because the menu clearly identifies dairy-free dishes, and there are several cakes and lunch options I can safely enjoy rather than simply settling for tea. It is slightly out of my way, but having a real choice makes the journey worthwhile.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My train arrives in town about twenty minutes before my morning class begins. That's not enough time to go shopping or visit friends, but it is too long to stand outside in winter. The café I use is immediately opposite the station and on the same road as my college, so I can have breakfast there without risking being late. The coffee is fine, although I probably wouldn't make a special trip for it; it's the location that makes me a regular customer.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: There are more fashionable cafés in the town centre, with impressive cakes and modern furniture, but I keep returning to a small family-run place. I started going there when I was new in the area. The owners remembered my name after two visits, ask how my week has gone and always make time to chat if I'm alone. Sometimes I go in intending only to buy a takeaway drink and end up staying for half an hour because the atmosphere is so friendly.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I look after my young nephew on Saturday mornings, and taking him to indoor cafés can be difficult because he gets bored quickly. I found one at the edge of a local park. Its large garden has tables under trees and plenty of safe space nearby, so he can move around and watch birds while I sit outside with a drink. In bad weather we don't go, but on warm mornings it is much more relaxing than trying to keep him quiet indoors.

options.text: It sells food suitable for their diet.
It offers a place to work quietly.
Its staff make customers feel welcome.
It has an attractive outside area.
It is convenient for their journey.
Its prices are lower than elsewhere.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker chooses the café because its quiet upstairs space is suitable for studying.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker values a café that provides several clearly marked dairy-free choices.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The café fits conveniently between the speaker's railway station and college.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker returns because the owners provide a personal and welcoming atmosphere.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The outdoor setting allows the speaker to relax while her young nephew has space nearby.
